title: Add Budget Siege challenge mode — CFO cuts budget every 30s, degrade gracefully
description: Innovation idea rated HIGH CONVICTION but never made into a task. Mode: user's architecture is running fine, then budget shrinks by 20% every 30 seconds. User must decide: which component gets downgraded first? When do you merge services? At what point does the system break? Scored on: how long the system stays alive, user experience degradation rate, final cost. Teaches cost-performance tradeoffs viscerally — the exact skill interviewers test when asking 'how would you reduce costs?'
acceptanceCriteria: Budget decreases by 20% every 30 seconds during simulation
User can downgrade/remove components to stay within budget
System metrics degrade as components are removed
Score: time survived, UX degradation, final architecture quality
3 template-specific budget siege scenarios (URL Shortener, Netflix, Uber)

title: Add Architecture Escape Room — puzzle-based sequential debugging challenges
description: Innovation idea rated HIGH CONVICTION. User is 'trapped' inside a broken system. Each room is a component with a specific failure. Fix the failure to unlock the next room. Rooms: (1) Database room — connection pool exhausted, solve by configuring max connections. (2) Cache room — stampede on expiry, solve by adding jitter to TTL. (3) Queue room — consumer lag, solve by adding consumers. (4) API room — no rate limiting, solve by adding token bucket. Time pressure adds urgency. 5 initial escape rooms.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 escape room scenarios with sequential puzzle structure
Each room presents a specific failure with 2-3 solution options
Wrong solutions explain WHY they don't work
Correct solutions unlock next room with celebration animation
Timer tracks total escape time for leaderboard

title: Add Choose Your Own Adventure branching scenarios during design
description: Innovation idea rated HIGH CONVICTION. During a design challenge, branch points appear: 'Your traffic just 10x'd. Do you (A) Add more app servers, (B) Add a cache layer, or (C) Switch to async processing?' Each choice leads to different simulation outcomes. The system shows consequences of each choice. 5 initial branching scenarios across different templates.
acceptanceCriteria: Branch point notifications appear mid-design with 2-3 options
Each option leads to visibly different simulation outcomes
After choosing, simulation shows WHY that choice helped or hurt
Can replay and choose differently to compare outcomes
5 branching scenarios across URL Shortener, Chat, Payment, Netflix, Uber

title: Add Packet's Journey — first-person narrated HTTP request traversal
description: Innovation idea rated HIGH CONVICTION. User IS an HTTP request. First-person narration: 'I arrive at the Load Balancer. It looks at my headers and decides to send me to Server 3 because it has the fewest active connections.' Visual: animated dot with speech bubbles at each hop. If the system is poorly designed, the journey is miserable — long waits, timeouts, retries. Builds empathy for the request path. 3 journey types: happy path, cache miss, error cascade.
acceptanceCriteria: Animated dot traverses architecture with first-person narration bubbles
Each hop explains WHY the component made its decision
3 journey types with different narratives and visual moods
Journey can be paused at any hop to inspect component state
Works on any loaded template (generates journey from topology)

title: Add Expert Replay — watch an expert design step-by-step with pause and commentary
description: Innovation idea from convergence. For each template, record a 'golden path' design sequence: expert adds components one by one with commentary explaining each decision. User watches the replay, can pause at any step, and sees the expert's reasoning. Based on Cognitive Apprenticeship model — experts externalize their process so novices can learn. 5 initial expert replays for top templates.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 expert replay recordings (1 per top template)
Each step adds/connects a component with text commentary
User can play/pause/step through the replay
Commentary explains WHY each component was chosen
After watching, user can attempt the same design from scratch

title: Add bundle size monitoring and Lighthouse CI performance budgets
description: No performance regression detection exists. Add: (1) Bundle analyzer in build pipeline showing per-module chunk sizes, (2) Lighthouse CI running on every PR with budgets: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 200ms, CLS < 0.1, (3) Performance budget alerts: warn if any module chunk exceeds 200KB, (4) Bundle size tracking over time in CI artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: next build outputs per-module chunk sizes
Lighthouse CI runs on PRs with Core Web Vitals budgets
CI fails if any module chunk exceeds 200KB gzipped
Bundle size trend visible in CI dashboard/artifacts

title: Handle concurrent browser tabs — prevent localStorage race conditions
description: Two browser tabs open simultaneously can corrupt localStorage state. Tab A saves nodes, Tab B saves different nodes, Tab A reads Tab B's state. Add: (1) BroadcastChannel API to sync state between tabs, (2) Leader election — only one tab writes to storage, others read, (3) Conflict resolution — last-write-wins with visual notification 'Design updated in another tab. Reload?'
acceptanceCriteria: Two tabs open the same Architex instance without corrupting state
When Tab A modifies canvas, Tab B shows a notification
No localStorage race conditions when both tabs write simultaneously
Tab close properly releases leadership

title: Add color blindness support — pattern fills and shape coding alongside colors
description: 8% of men have color vision deficiency. Current visualizations rely on color alone for: node categories (13 colors), edge types (9 colors), heatmap gradient (green→red), particle types (9 colors), state indicators (6 colors). Add: (1) Pattern fills option (diagonal stripes, dots, crosshatch) as secondary encoding alongside color, (2) Shape distinctiveness already exists for nodes (8 shapes) — ensure it's sufficient, (3) High-contrast mode toggle that uses only 4 distinguishable colors + patterns, (4) Test with Sim Daltonism or Color Oracle.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessibility settings include 'Color blind friendly' toggle
When enabled, all color-coded elements add pattern fills as secondary encoding
Heatmap gradient adds numeric labels alongside color
Tested with protanopia and deuteranopia simulation tools
All information conveyed by color is also conveyed by shape/pattern/text

title: Add low-end device performance degradation — reduce visual fidelity on slow hardware
description: India/SE Asia market uses low-end phones where 2000 particles + heatmap + 50 nodes will lag. Add: (1) Use Compute Pressure API (or performance.now() frame timing) to detect slow devices, (2) Auto-reduce: particle count from 2000→500, disable heatmap overlay, simplify node rendering to text-only at lower threshold, (3) Manual toggle: 'Performance mode' in settings, (4) Target: 30fps minimum on 4-year-old mid-range phone.
acceptanceCriteria: Frame rate drops below 30fps triggers automatic quality reduction
Particle count reduced, heatmap disabled, node rendering simplified
Manual 'Performance mode' toggle in settings
Performance mode persisted across sessions
Canvas remains functional (all features work, just less pretty)

title: Add input size validation for large JSON template imports
description: DiffPanel and import flow accept JSON files without size limits. A 10MB JSON paste/import could freeze the browser. Add: (1) File size check on import: warn at 1MB, block at 5MB, (2) Node count validation: warn at 200 nodes, block at 500, (3) Paste validation: limit clipboard paste to 1MB, (4) Graceful error message: 'This design is too large. Maximum 500 components supported.'
acceptanceCriteria: Import warns at 1MB file size, blocks at 5MB with clear message
Node count validation: warns at 200, blocks at 500
Paste from clipboard validated for size before parsing
Error messages are user-friendly, not technical

title: Add error boundaries per-panel — Properties and BottomPanel crash isolation
description: Error boundaries exist per-node (withErrorBoundary on all 72 nodeTypes) but NOT per-panel. If PropertiesPanel or BottomPanel throws, the entire right/bottom section crashes. Add ErrorBoundary wrappers around: (1) PropertiesPanel content, (2) BottomPanel content, (3) SimulationDashboard, (4) Each bottom panel tab individually. Show 'Something went wrong — Retry' instead of white void.
acceptanceCriteria: PropertiesPanel crash shows retry button instead of white space
BottomPanel tab crash only affects that tab, not all tabs
SimulationDashboard crash doesn't take down the entire simulation UI
Each error boundary shows component name for debugging

title: Add auto-save indicator and manual save/restore functionality
description: Users have no visual confirmation that their work is saved. Canvas-store uses Zustand persist but no indicator shows save status. Add: (1) Auto-save indicator in status bar: 'Saved just now' / 'Saving...' / 'Save failed', (2) Manual save button (Cmd+S) with toast confirmation, (3) Named saves: 'Save as [name]' with multiple save slots, (4) Restore from saves list.
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar shows auto-save status (Saved/Saving/Failed)
Cmd+S triggers manual save with toast confirmation
Multiple named save slots available
Restore from any previous save without losing current work

title: Add simulation accessibility for screen readers — live narration of simulation state
description: Current a11y covers static canvas (CanvasDescription.tsx with aria-live). But during simulation, a blind user gets NO information about: particle flow, heatmap changes, metric updates, chaos events, bottleneck formation. Add: (1) aria-live region that narrates key simulation events: 'Simulation started. Traffic at 1000 RPS. Cache hit rate 95%.', (2) Periodic summary every 10 seconds: 'System healthy. Throughput 5000 RPS. No errors.', (3) Chaos event announcements: 'Cache failure injected. Error rate rising to 5%.', (4) Completion summary: 'Simulation complete. Peak latency 200ms. 3 bottlenecks detected.'
acceptanceCriteria: Screen reader announces simulation start/stop/pause
Key metrics announced periodically (every 10 seconds or on significant change)
Chaos events announced immediately
Bottleneck detection announced
Completion summary with key metrics

title: Create codebase architecture diagram for developer onboarding
description: 1000+ files, 13 modules, 34 simulation files, 13 stores — new developers can't navigate this. Create an interactive architecture diagram (using Architex itself!) showing: (1) Module boundaries, (2) Store relationships, (3) Simulation pipeline, (4) Component hierarchy, (5) Data flow from user input to screen. Store as both a template JSON (eat-your-own-dogfood) and a static Mermaid diagram in docs/.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture diagram in docs/architecture.md using Mermaid
Shows all 13 modules and their relationships
Shows store dependency graph
Shows simulation pipeline (10-stage)
New developer can understand the codebase structure in 10 minutes

title: Write 'How to add a new module' developer guide
description: 13 modules exist but no guide for adding module #14. Write docs/guides/adding-a-module.md covering: (1) Create wrapper component implementing ModuleContent interface, (2) Register in MODULE_COMPONENTS map in page.tsx, (3) Add to ModuleType union in types.ts, (4) Add activity bar icon, (5) Add keyboard shortcut, (6) Add to command palette, (7) Add store if needed, (8) Add to cross-module bridge registry. Include a worked example.
acceptanceCriteria: Step-by-step guide from zero to working module
References exact files and line numbers to modify
Includes a minimal worked example
Covers: wrapper, registration, types, navigation, store, bridges
A developer can add a new module in under 2 hours following the guide

title: Write 'How to add a simulation engine feature' developer guide
description: 34 simulation files with complex inter-dependencies but no guide for extending the simulation. Write docs/guides/extending-simulation.md covering: (1) 10-stage pipeline overview, (2) How to add a new chaos event type, (3) How to add a new pressure counter, (4) How to add a new issue type, (5) How to add a new topology profile, (6) How to add a new narrative template. Include code examples for each.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide covers all 6 extension points with code examples
Pipeline diagram showing where each extension point fits
A developer can add a new chaos event in under 30 minutes following the guide
References simulation-orchestrator.ts pipeline stages

title: Add Storybook stories for top 10 canvas components
description: Only BaseNode.stories.tsx exists. No visual component catalog for the other 20+ canvas components. Add Storybook stories for: (1) DataFlowEdge (9 edge types), (2) SimulationDashboard (running/paused states), (3) ChaosQuickBar (expanded/collapsed), (4) EmptyState, (5) NodeContextMenu, (6) HeatmapOverlay (3 metrics), (7) TimeTravelScrubber (with breakpoints), (8) WhatIfPanel, (9) CanvasToolbar (wide/compact), (10) ComponentPalette (search/filter). Each story shows all visual states.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 new Storybook story files created
Each story shows all visual states (idle, active, error, loading)
Stories are interactive (controls for props)
Storybook builds without errors

title: Add E2E test suite with Playwright for critical user flows
description: Zero E2E tests exist. All browser testing is manual. Add Playwright E2E tests for the 5 most critical flows: (1) Load template → verify nodes render, (2) Start simulation → verify dashboard appears, (3) Drag node from palette → verify node created, (4) Open command palette → switch module → switch back, (5) Export as JSON → re-import → verify identical. Run in CI on every PR.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 Playwright E2E test files created
Tests run in CI (GitHub Actions) on every PR
Tests pass on Chrome and Firefox
Total E2E suite completes in under 60 seconds
Screenshots captured on failure for debugging

title: Add RTL (right-to-left) language support for Arabic/Hebrew users
description: Entire UI is LTR only. For Arabic/Hebrew markets, add: (1) dir='rtl' support on root layout, (2) Flip sidebar to right side, properties to left, (3) Mirror all directional CSS (margins, paddings, flex-direction), (4) Use CSS logical properties (margin-inline-start instead of margin-left), (5) Test with Arabic locale. Use Tailwind RTL plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding dir='rtl' to html tag correctly mirrors the entire layout
Sidebar appears on right, properties on left in RTL mode
All directional CSS uses logical properties (inline-start/end)
Canvas remains LTR (diagrams are universal)
Text alignment correct in RTL

title: Activate PostHog analytics with key user behavior events
description: PostHog analytics infrastructure is ready (AnalyticsProvider.tsx, posthog.ts) but not activated — needs API key. Define and track these events: (1) template_loaded (which template, difficulty), (2) simulation_started / simulation_completed (duration, chaos events used), (3) challenge_attempted / challenge_completed (score, time, hints used), (4) module_switched (from, to), (5) export_used (format), (6) feature_discovered (first use of heatmap, chaos, what-if). These inform product decisions about what users actually use.
acceptanceCriteria: PostHog initialized with API key (env variable)
10+ key events tracked with relevant properties
Events fire correctly (verified in PostHog dashboard)
No PII in event properties (no names, emails, API keys)
Event tracking respects user consent/do-not-track

title: Add template validation to CI pipeline — prevent broken templates from merging
description: Templates can be committed with invalid JSON, missing required fields, or references to non-existent node IDs (found in Notification System and Twitter simulation chaos scenarios). Add CI validation: (1) JSON schema validation against DiagramTemplate type, (2) Node ID reference check — all simulation chaos targetNodes exist in template, (3) Difficulty range check (1-5), (4) LearnSteps count check (minimum 3 for difficulty >= 3), (5) Run on every PR that touches templates/.
acceptanceCriteria: CI step validates all template JSON files on every PR
Catches: missing fields, invalid difficulty, orphan node references
Clear error messages with file:field path
PR blocked if any template is invalid
Existing invalid templates (Notification, Twitter sim refs) caught and documented

title: Add long node label overflow handling — truncate with tooltip
description: Node labels are rendered with `whitespace-nowrap` in BaseNode.tsx:470. A label like 'Machine Learning Inference Service with GPU Acceleration' would overflow the 260px max-width. Add: (1) Text truncation with ellipsis at max-width, (2) Full label shown in tooltip on hover, (3) Simplified LOD label also truncated, (4) Test with 50+ character labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Labels longer than node width truncate with ellipsis
Tooltip shows full label on hover
Truncation works at all 3 LOD tiers
No horizontal overflow or layout breaking

title: Fix 3 eslint-disable comments in production canvas code — BottomPanel, TimeTravelScrubber, HeatmapOverlay
description: 3 eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps comments in production code: BottomPanel.tsx:78, TimeTravelScrubber.tsx:207, HeatmapOverlay.tsx:190. Each suppresses a hooks dependency warning. Fix the underlying deps issue instead of suppressing.
acceptanceCriteria: All 3 eslint-disable comments removed
Hooks deps arrays corrected
No functional regression

title: Replace 16 as-any casts in SystemDesignNodes.test.tsx with proper typed props
description: SystemDesignNodes.test.tsx uses (props as any) 16 times to render node components. Create a properly typed makeNodeProps() helper that satisfies NodeProps<SystemDesignNode> without any casts.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero as-any casts in test file
Typed makeNodeProps helper created
All tests still pass

title: Test 1-node architecture edge case — single node with no edges on canvas
description: No task tests the single-node edge case. A canvas with 1 node and 0 edges should: (1) render the node correctly, (2) show properties when clicked, (3) NOT crash simulation (should show warning instead), (4) export correctly as JSON/PNG. Verify and add test.
acceptanceCriteria: 1-node canvas renders without errors
Simulation on 1 node shows warning not crash
Export produces valid output for single node

title: Test browser zoom 150%+ layout — ensure no overflow or clipping
description: Browser zoom (Cmd++) is different from canvas zoom. At 150%+ browser zoom, toolbar buttons may overflow, sidebar may clip, status bar text may truncate. Test at 125%, 150%, 200% browser zoom and fix any layout issues. Use CSS clamp() or responsive units where needed.
acceptanceCriteria: No layout overflow at 150% browser zoom
All toolbar buttons accessible
Status bar text readable
Sidebar scrollable if needed
